root_text.sn47.14:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā vajjīsu viharati ukkacelāyaṁ gaṅgāya nadiyā tīre mahatā bhikkhusaṅghena saddhiṁ aciraparinibbutesu sāriputtamoggallānesu.
root_text.sn47.14:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena bhagavā bhikkhusaṅghaparivuto ajjhokāse nisinno hoti.
root_text.sn47.14:2.1: Atha kho bhagavā tuṇhībhūtaṁ bhikkhusaṅghaṁ anuviloketvā bhikkhū āmantesi:
root_text.sn47.14:2.2: “api myāyaṁ, bhikkhave, parisā suññā viya khāyati parinibbutesu sāriputtamoggallānesu.
root_text.sn47.14:2.3: Asuññā me, bhikkhave, parisā hoti, anapekkhā tassaṁ disāyaṁ hoti, yassaṁ disāyaṁ sāriputtamoggallānā viharanti.
root_text.sn47.14:2.4: Ye hi te, bhikkhave, ahesuṁ atītamaddhānaṁ arahanto sammāsambuddhā, tesampi bhagavantānaṁ etapparamaṁyeva sāvakayugaṁ ahosi—
root_text.sn47.14:2.6: Yepi te, bhikkhave, bhavissanti anāgatamaddhānaṁ arahanto sammāsambuddhā, tesampi bhagavantānaṁ etapparamaṁyeva sāvakayugaṁ bhavissati—seyyathāpi mayhaṁ sāriputtamoggallānā.
root_text.sn47.14:2.7: Acchariyaṁ, bhikkhave, sāvakānaṁ, abbhutaṁ, bhikkhave, sāvakānaṁ.
root_text.sn47.14:2.8: Satthu ca nāma sāsanakarā bhavissanti ovādappaṭikarā, catunnañca parisānaṁ piyā bhavissanti manāpā garubhāvanīyā ca.
root_text.sn47.14:2.9: Acchariyaṁ, bhikkhave, tathāgatassa, abbhutaṁ, bhikkhave, tathāgatassa.
root_text.sn47.14:2.10: Evarūpepi nāma sāvakayuge parinibbute natthi tathāgatassa soko vā paridevo vā.
root_text.sn47.14:2.11: Taṁ kutettha, bhikkhave, labbhā. Yaṁ taṁ jātaṁ bhūtaṁ saṅkhataṁ palokadhammaṁ, taṁ vata mā palujjīti—
root_text.sn47.14:2.13: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, mahato rukkhassa tiṭṭhato sāravato ye mahantatarā khandhā te palujjeyyuṁ;
root_text.sn47.14:2.14: evameva kho, bhikkhave, mahato bhikkhusaṅghassa tiṭṭhato sāravato sāriputtamoggallānā parinibbutā.
root_text.sn47.14:2.15: Taṁ kutettha, bhikkhave, labbhā. Yaṁ taṁ jātaṁ bhūtaṁ saṅkhataṁ palokadhammaṁ, taṁ vata mā palujjīti—
root_text.sn47.14:2.17: Tasmātiha, bhikkhave, attadīpā viharatha attasaraṇā anaññasaraṇā, dhammadīpā dhammasaraṇā anaññasaraṇā.
root_text.sn47.14:3.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu attadīpo viharati attasaraṇo anaññasaraṇo, dhammadīpo dhammasaraṇo anaññasaraṇo?
root_text.sn47.14:3.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kāye kāyānupassī viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ;
root_text.sn47.14:3.5: dhammesu dhammānupassī viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ.
root_text.sn47.14:3.6: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu attadīpo viharati attasaraṇo anaññasaraṇo, dhammadīpo dhammasaraṇo anaññasaraṇo.
root_text.sn47.14:3.7: Ye hi keci, bhikkhave, etarahi vā mamaccaye vā attadīpā viharissanti attasaraṇā anaññasaraṇā, dhammadīpā dhammasaraṇā anaññasaraṇā; tamatagge mete, bhikkhave, bhikkhū bhavissanti ye keci sikkhākāmā”ti.

translation_text.sn47.14:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying in the land of the Vajjis near Ukkacelā on the bank of the Ganges river, together with a large Saṅgha of mendicants. It was not long after Sāriputta and Moggallāna had become fully quenched.
translation_text.sn47.14:1.2: Now, at that time the Buddha was sitting in the open, surrounded by the Saṅgha of mendicants.
translation_text.sn47.14:2.1: Then the Buddha looked around the Saṅgha of mendicants, who were silent. He addressed them:
translation_text.sn47.14:2.2: “Mendicants, this assembly seems empty to me now that Sāriputta and Moggallāna have become fully quenched.
translation_text.sn47.14:2.3: When Sāriputta and Moggallāna were alive, my assembly was never empty; I had no concern for any region where they stayed.
translation_text.sn47.14:2.4: The Buddhas of the past or the future have pairs of chief disciples who are no better than
translation_text.sn47.14:2.5: Sāriputta and Moggallāna were to me.
translation_text.sn47.14:2.7: It’s an incredible and amazing quality of such disciples
translation_text.sn47.14:2.8: that they fulfill the Teacher’s instructions and follow his advice. And they’re liked and approved, respected and admired by the four assemblies.
translation_text.sn47.14:2.9: And it’s an incredible and amazing quality of the Realized One
translation_text.sn47.14:2.10: that when such a pair of disciples becomes fully quenched he does not sorrow or lament.
translation_text.sn47.14:2.11: How could it possibly be so that what is born, created, conditioned, and liable to wear out should not wear out?
translation_text.sn47.14:2.13: Suppose there was a large tree standing with heartwood, and the largest branches broke off.
translation_text.sn47.14:2.14: In the same way, in the great Saṅgha that stands with heartwood, Sāriputta and Moggallāna have become fully quenched.
translation_text.sn47.14:2.15: How could it possibly be so that what is born, created, conditioned, and liable to wear out should not wear out?
translation_text.sn47.14:2.17: So mendicants, live as your own island, your own refuge, with no other refuge. Let the teaching be your island and your refuge, with no other refuge.
translation_text.sn47.14:3.1: And how does a mendicant do this?
translation_text.sn47.14:3.2: It’s when a mendicant meditates by observing an aspect of the body—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn47.14:3.3: They meditate observing an aspect of feelings …
translation_text.sn47.14:3.5: principles—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn47.14:3.6: That’s how a mendicant lives as their own island, their own refuge, with no other refuge. That’s how the teaching is their island and their refuge, with no other refuge.
translation_text.sn47.14:3.7: Whether now or after I have passed, any who shall live as their own island, their own refuge, with no other refuge; with the teaching as their island and their refuge, with no other refuge—those mendicants of mine who want to train shall be among the best of the best.”

variant_text.sn47.14:2.4: hi → ye’pi (bj, sya-all) | etapparamaṁyeva → etaparamaṁyeva (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed)
